message:
fix(widgets): respect NoStyling degradation in help, history_panel, and constraint overlay

A coordinated cleanup across three widgets that all had the same
latent bug: they checked DegradationLevel::apply_styling() inside a
conditional that short-circuited entire render branches, which meant
that under NoStyling — where we want content rendered without colors
— either a fallback path ran *different* layout code from the styled
path, or the fallback skipped parts of the output entirely.  The fix
is consistent: compute per-role Style values up front (the real
style when apply_styling() is true, Style::default() otherwise) and
then run a single render path that uses them.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/help.rs

- Short-mode render: the ellipsis-drawing branch was gated on
  `ell_total <= space_left && deg.apply_styling()`, so the "...
  more" indicator vanished in NoStyling mode.  Now we compute
  `let ellipsis_style = if deg.apply_styling() { self.separator_style }
                        else { Style::default() };`
  and draw the ellipsis unconditionally, just with a default style
  when styling is off.
- Full-mode render: previously two completely separate layouts:
    styled   → draw key with key_style, pad to max_key_w, draw desc
               with desc_style.
    unstyled → format!("{:>width$}  {}", entry.key, entry.desc,
               width = max_key_w) — which *right-aligns* the key
               column.
  That meant a single `q` entry showed up flush-right against the
  gap column in NoStyling mode, drifting visually compared to the
  left-aligned styled mode.  Now we compute key_style / desc_style
  up front (real style or Style::default()) and use the same
  left-aligned, per-cell draw path in both modes.
- New tests:
    render_short_truncation_keeps_ellipsis_without_styling —
        asserts the '…' glyph is drawn on the row even when
        degradation is NoStyling.
    render_full_no_styling_keeps_left_aligned_key_column —
        builds a Full mode help with "q"/"ctrl+s", renders under
        NoStyling, and asserts the short key is still at column 0
        (not right-aligned to max_key_w).  Also asserts the first
        character of 'ctrl+s' on the next row is at the same x
        column.
    render_full_no_styling_uses_display_width_for_wide_keys —
        builds a Full mode help with "🦀" (width 2) and "ctrl+s",
        renders under NoStyling, and asserts the alignment uses
        display width rather than byte length.
- New test helpers row_text(&Buffer, y, width) -> String and
  find_char_column(&Buffer, y, width, char) -> Option<usize> so
  these degradation tests can inspect the rendered buffer without
  re-implementing the walk in each test.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/history_panel.rs

- render() now takes a snapshot of deg = frame.buffer.degradation
  at the top and short-circuits when !deg.render_content().  The
  old code always rendered, even when the degradation level
  explicitly said "skip content".
- Four per-role styles (title_style, undo_style, redo_style,
  marker_style) are pre-computed from the corresponding
  self.*_style fields, swapping in Style::default() when
  apply_styling() is false.  The rest of the render path uses the
  local variables instead of self.*_style directly, so the
  "NoStyling but still render" case draws the same structural
  content without colors.
- Same fix applied to the "hidden N more" summary line and the
  per-entry draw_text_span calls.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/constraint_overlay.rs

- ConstraintOverlayStyle gains a respected show_size_diff flag
  (previously the field existed on the style struct but the
  format_label code path ignored it).  When show_size_diff is
  false, the "→ WxH" arrow section is suppressed even if the
  requested and received areas differ.
- New test format_label_hides_size_diff_when_disabled builds a
  ConstraintOverlayStyle { show_size_diff: false, .. }, renders a
  label with mismatched requested/received rects, and asserts the
  output contains the received dimensions ("8x4") but not the
  arrow glyph '→' and not the requested dimensions ("10x5").

Together these three fixes mean that under NoStyling — which is
what accessibility tools and CI snapshot tests use — widgets render
the same layout as under the full styled path, just without color
codes.  Before this commit, NoStyling mode would silently drop
ellipses, right-align help-mode key columns, and fail to honor
show_size_diff on constraint overlays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
